Play and attack right side of field

Play and attack left side of field

Lineout plays — A and B variants

facets.subfacets: Line speed · Tackle technique · Defensive shape · Spacing · Dominant collision · Chop tackle · Blitz/drift · Communication
facets.notes: Missed tackles and spacing errors lose games faster than poor attack. Must drill daily. Non-negotiable.

facets.subfacets: Forward pod structure · Backline alignment · Strike plays · Phase continuity · Edge attack · Middle-field manipulation · Shape transitions
facets.notes: Teams spend 60-70% of possession time in phase organisation. Shape fluency reduces hesitation under fatigue.

facets.subfacets: Arrival timing · Cleanout technique · Ball presentation · Ruck spacing · Jackal systems · Counter-ruck · Fast-ball generation
facets.notes: Breakdown dictates tempo, momentum, possession quality and fatigue accumulation. Non-negotiable weekly focus.

facets.subfacets: Scrum shape and binding · Engagement timing · Throwing accuracy · Jump/lift mechanics · Calling systems · Maul setup and defence · Defensive lineout
facets.notes: Set piece determines launch quality, territory, penalties and psychological control. Dominance here wins field position.

facets.subfacets: Exit structure · Box kick systems · Touch finding · Grubber/chip · Kick receipt · Restart kicks · Territorial chess · 50/22 trigger recognition
facets.notes: Rugby is territory-driven. Poor exits destroy defensive energy and invite sustained pressure phases.

facets.subfacets: Aerobic base · Repeat sprint ability · Contact conditioning · Match fitness · Collision volume under fatigue · Recovery capacity
facets.notes: Must be maintained weekly in-season but not overloaded. Integrate within contact drills where possible.

facets.subfacets: Kickoff receipt structures · Aerial contest · Contestable restarts · Deep territorial restarts · Exit shape · Chase pod organisation
facets.notes: Restart ownership creates momentum swings and territorial pressure. 4-5 restarts per match — each one matters.

facets.subfacets: Playing with a lead · Chasing game · 14-man rugby · Wet weather · Final five minutes · Clock manipulation · One-score scenarios
facets.notes: Clutch moments decide elite matches. Teams must rehearse pressure scenarios weekly to reduce decision hesitation.

facets.subfacets: Scrumhalf service · Flyhalf kicking game · Lineout calling · Back-three aerial · Front-row mobility · Loosie breakdown roles
facets.notes: Small refinements maintain technical sharpness. Best integrated into unit sessions within set piece and attack blocks.

facets.subfacets: Turnover attack launch · Counterattack structure · Scramble defence · Broken field scanning · Reconnection speed
facets.notes: Broken-field chaos creates high-value tries and catastrophic defensive risks. Drill recognition, not systems.

facets.subfacets: Penalty reduction · High tackle prevention · Referee picture management · Captain communication · Emotional discipline
facets.notes: Integrated throughout all training. Address patterns early in the week based on referee tendency and last match data.

facets.subfacets: Pressure handling · Resilience · Team talks · Accountability · Emotional regulation · Confidence · Leadership group activation
facets.notes: Present daily in culture and expectation. Rarely dominates field session time. Embedded in standards and rituals.

planner.activity: Cold/contrast therapy, static stretching, mobility work, hydration protocol
Full-game review: tackle completion, breakdown speed, exits, set-piece. Focus on patterns not individual errors
Scrum and lineout clip review. Binding faults, jumping mechanics, maul exits
Attack shape review: depth, alignment, decision timing. Kick receipt and exit execution
Weekly debrief: culture, standards, captain accountability, team goal alignment. Agenda set by captain

planner.label: TUESDAY  —  FIELD SESSION 1  |  DEFENCE · BREAKDOWN · CONDITIONING  (120 min)

planner.activity: SAQ runs · passing pairs · passing under pressure · dynamic stretch · band activation
Fast tempo touch — focus on communication calls and line speed triggering
Clarity session: weekly goal, opposition insight, defensive call of the week

planner.facet: DEFENCE  —  Tackle Technique and Defensive Shape
planner.activity: Chop tackle reps · dominant tackle drill · 2v1 defensive spacing · blitz/drift trigger sets · communication under fatigue
Cleanout body position · seal technique · jackal timing and clamp · counter-ruck reps · ball presentation under pressure
Hydration · brief tactical reminder · energy reset
Hit-and-reload sets · carry-into-contact conditioning · tackle-and-reload circuit · collision under fatigue
Full defensive line walk-through · then live reps at 70% pace · fold system · communication sequences
Scramble defence after turnover · reconnection speed · counterattack launch off turnover ball
Static stretch · positional accountability chat · brief review of key moments
Full-body warmup · thoracic and hip mobility · shoulder activation · scrum-specific prep
Squat/deadlift/hinge patterns · upper push/pull · explosive plyometric work · scrum-specific loading
Short-space repeat sprint sets · bag hits · collision-to-sprint sequences · work:rest ratio management
Foam roll · static stretching · hydration · session debrief by S&C coach
Brief tactical reminder for Thursday. Confirm lineout calls. Confirm match plan key points

planner.label: THURSDAY  —  FIELD SESSION 2  |  ATTACK · SET PIECE · KICKING · SCENARIOS  (120 min)

planner.activity: Catch-pass fluency · short/long pass reps · skip and flat pass · catch under pressure · SAQ gates
Structured touch: enforce depth, width, and strike play calls. No aimless passing
Key opponent tendencies: set-piece, kick patterns, defensive alignment. Set weekly game cues

planner.facet: ATTACK SHAPE — Phase Play and Strike Plays
planner.activity: Shape reps off scrum and lineout · M-plays · bus/taxi pods · strike play timing · edge exploitation · skip pass from 10
Tight-five scrum reps · binding and timing · lineout calling reps · INDIA/JEKWA options · maul setup and exit · defensive lineout contest
Hydration · visual tactical board · call confirmation

planner.facet: TACTICAL KICKING — Exit Systems and Kick Receipt
planner.activity: Exit structure reps · box kick with chase pod · kick receipt formation · grubber and chip execution · 50/22 trigger recognition
3-man pod receipt · contestable kickoff structure · exit shape · chase alignment
5-metre attack sequences · pick-and-go vs power play · goal line defence reps · choke tackle attempt
One-score game · chasing last 10 minutes · 14-man rugby · wet-weather exit · quick restart exploitation
Brief collective debrief · accountability call-out · top performers acknowledged · Friday plan confirmed

planner.label: FRIDAY  —  CAPTAIN'S RUN  |  CLARITY SESSION  (45–60 min)

planner.activity: Light SAQ · no contact · muscle activation only · mental sharpness focus
Defensive call confirmation · spacing walk · fold system walk · no live contact
Lineout call walk · scrum sequence confirm · restart pod walk
Three key strike plays walked at half pace · call words confirmed · eye movement drilled
Final trigger review: clock kill · exit call · 14-man structure · quick restart intent
Opposition brief · game model reminder · season goal anchor · identity statement · captain's words
Personal activation · mental prep · nutrition and hydration · jersey-on time
Scrum engagement · lineout lifts · binding check · contact activation
Passing reps · kick receipt · strike play run-through · defensive trigger walk
Structured team run · defensive walk · restart walk · final mental cues
Emotional activation · identity anchor · captain's call · game model final word
Performance data review · tactical correction · emotional reset · re-motivation
Result acknowledgement · effort recognition · key observations · recovery plan
Pool session or light walk · static mobility · cold therapy · hydration loading
Analyst pulls match clips: defence errors, breakdown speed, exit failures, set-piece outcomes
Next week plan: what to fix, what to build, opposition analysis begins

planner.label: "The team that executes simple systems at extreme speed, physicality and accuracy usually beats the team with the most creative whiteboard.  The game punishes complexity under fatigue.  The purpose of weekly coaching is not to install more information — it is to reduce hesitation under pressure."
